name: Transpose Then Reverse Rows
summary: Mirror across the diagonal, then reverse each row â€” a 90-degree clockwise rotation in place.
visualizationIntent: init: the matrix is shown with its main diagonal marked special (the mirror axis); loop(transpose): each above-diagonal cell (row,col) is current and its mirror (col,row) is compared as the two swap and flash; loop(reverse): each row's cells flash as the row is reversed left-to-right; return: matrix holds the rotated image.

lineExplanations.3: Measure n, the number of rows and columns.
lineExplanations.5: Walk the columns to the right of the diagonal only.
lineExplanations.6: Swap this cell with its mirror across the diagonal (transpose).
lineExplanations.7: Walk over each row of the (now transposed) matrix.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def rotate(self, matrix): the method receiving the n x n matrix.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(matrix) is the number of rows, n.
syntaxExplanations.4: for row in range(n) iterates the row indices.
syntaxExplanations.5: range(row + 1, n) skips the diagonal and lower triangle so each pair swaps once.
syntaxExplanations.6: a, b = b, a swaps two cells in a single statement.
syntaxExplanations.7: for current_row in matrix iterates the actual row lists.
syntaxExplanations.8: current_row.reverse() reverses that row in place.
syntaxExplanations.9: return matrix hands back the rotated grid.
